evals.prompt: Show me everything Cursor is doing on my machine right now - I want a live view that updates every second and shows subagents under their parent agents.
evals.expected_output: The assistant invokes the cursor-top CLI in its default interactive mode and explains how to expand/collapse the hierarchy. It does not modify any Cursor data.
assertions: The assistant runs `cursor-top` (interactive mode) or instructs the user to run it
When `cursor-top` is not on PATH and `dist/cli.js` is missing, the assistant directs the user to run `/zoto-cursor-top-init` once before retrying
The assistant explains the keyboard shortcuts for expand, collapse, and quit
The assistant mentions that each row shows model, repo, start time, and the last 3 log lines
The assistant does not modify any Cursor data directory
The default refresh interval (1 second) is mentioned or used unmodified

evals.prompt: I'd like to drop a snapshot of the current Cursor agents into a status report - give me a one-shot output I can paste in.
evals.expected_output: The assistant runs cursor-top with --once (and optionally --json) so the command exits after one frame, and explains the difference between the two output modes.
assertions: The assistant uses `--once` to render a single frame
The assistant offers `--json` as an alternative machine-readable mode
The assistant clarifies that the CLI exits cleanly so it can be embedded in a report
The assistant does not start interactive mode (no need for keyboard navigation in this scenario)

evals.prompt: I want to take a screenshot of cursor-top for documentation but I don't currently have any Cursor sessions running. How do I make the UI look populated?
evals.expected_output: The assistant launches cursor-top in demo mode so the user sees a populated, scrolling fixture without needing any real Cursor processes.
assertions: The assistant explains that demo mode uses synthetic data and that log lines scroll on every tick
The assistant notes that demo data is not coming from real Cursor processes
The assistant does not require the user to start a Cursor session first
